---
title: "hw2"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## load packages
```{r}
library(dataRetrieval)
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(dplyr)
```
# get water temperature data from the Willametter River from NWIS server
```{r}
siteNumbers <- c("14211720", "14211010", "14166000")
parameterCd <- "00010"  # water temperature
startDate <- "2010-01-01"
endDate <- "2023-12-31"

temp_data <- readNWISdv(siteNumbers, parameterCd, startDate, endDate)
```

# option to load data directly instead
```{r}
temp_data <- read.csv(here("river_temp_data.csv"))
```


clean the data
```{r}
# rename columns for clarity
colnames(temp_data) <- c("agency_cd", "site_no", "Date", "temp_C")
temp_data <- subset(temp_data, select = -5)
# extract some date info for use later
temp_data$month = month(temp_data$Date, label=TRUE)
temp_data$year = year(temp_data$Date)
```

plot the data
```{r}
# check by plotting
ggplot(temp_data, aes(x=Date, y=temp_C, color=site_no)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title="Stream Temperature at Willamette River Sites",
       x="Date", y="Temperature (°C)", color="Site Number") +
  theme_minimal()
```

Calculate summary statistics
```{r}
temp_data |>
  group_by(site_no) |>
  summarize(mean = mean(temp_C),
            min = min(temp_C),
            max = max(temp_C),
            stdev = sd(temp_C))
```


store the data
```{r}
write.csv(temp_data, here("river_temp_data.csv"))
```
